indication: Acute pulmonary edema — assess for a primary cardiac cause.
technique: Limited bedside 2D transthoracic echocardiogram with Doppler.
findings: Preserved left ventricular systolic function (EF ~60%). No regional wall-motion abnormality. No significant valvular stenosis or regurgitation. Normal right ventricular size and function. No pericardial effusion. Inferior vena cava without plethora at the time of study (post-diuresis).
impression: No primary structural or systolic cardiac cause for the acute pulmonary edema (normal EF, no valvular or regional abnormality). The picture is most consistent with an acute volume/circulatory insult in a patient with limited capacity to handle a fluid load, rather than new heart failure.
